changes_made: Changed k8s_pod_persistent_volume_backup_enabled to k8s_persistentvolume_backup_enabled for consistency with resource naming
Changed k8s_pod_persistent_volume_snapshot_enabled to k8s_persistentvolume_snapshot_enabled for consistency with resource naming
review_notes: The control CP-6 (3) is applicable to Kubernetes as it involves ensuring data resilience through backups and snapshots. The original function names were mostly accurate but needed adjustments for consistency with Kubernetes resource naming conventions. Specifically, 'persistentvolume' should be used as the resource name instead of 'pod' when referring to persistent volume operations.
naming_improvements: Use 'persistentvolume' instead of 'pod_persistent_volume' to align with Kubernetes resource naming conventions
title: Alternate Storage Site | Accessibility
step1_reasoning: The control CP-6 (3) focuses on ensuring that alternate storage sites are accessible, which translates to ensuring that data stored in Kubernetes is backed up and can be restored. In Kubernetes, this involves ensuring that etcd, which stores cluster state, is backed up, and that persistent volumes used by pods have backup and snapshot capabilities. These checks ensure data resilience and accessibility in case of failure.
